project.title: CX-MS of merozoite surface protein-1
project.projectDescription: The merozoite surface protein-1 (MSP-1) is the most abundant protein on the surface of the erythrocyte-invading Plasmodium merozoite, the causative agent of malaria. MSP-1 is essential for merozoite formation, entry into and escape from erythrocytes, and is a promising vaccine candidate. We solved monomeric and dimeric structures of full-length MSP-1 construct MSP-1D as previously used in a successful phase 1a clinical trial (Blank et al. 2020). CS-MS was used to validate the de novo built atomic model.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: MSP-1D was incubated with a 20-fold molar excess of DSSO for 60 minutes at room temperature before stopping the reaction with quenching buffer. Samples were digested in urea lysis buffer (8 M urea, 100 mM NaCl, 50 mM TEAB pH 8.5) using a two stage Lys-C and trypsin digestion protocol. Samples were reduced and alkylated using 10 mM DTT (1 h, 27 °C) and 30 mM Iodacetamide (30 min, room temperature, in the dark). Lys-C was added at a 1:100 enzyme-to-protein molar ratio and incubated at 37 °C for 4 h. Afterwards the sample was diluted 1:5 using 50 mM TEAB. Trypsin was added at a 1:50 enzyme-to-protein molar ratio and samples were incubated overnight at 37 °C. Digestion was stopped by acidification (0.6% v/v TFA) prior to desalting using StageTips. Dried peptides were resuspended in reconstitution buffer (2.5% 1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoro-2-propanol, 0.1% TFA in water) prior to LC-MS measurement, which was conducted using an Ultimate 3000 UPLC (Thermo Fisher Scientific) coupled to an Q-Exactive HF-X mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific). During the LC separation, the peptides were first loaded onto a trapping cartridge (Acclaim PepMap300 C18, 5 µm, 300 Å wide pore, Thermo Fisher Scientific) and washed for 3 min with 0.1% TFA in water. Analytical separation was performed using a nanoEase MZ Peptide analytical column (300 Å, 1.7 µm, 75 µm x 200 mm, Waters) and carried out for 90 min total analysis time. The 90 min LC method consisted of a multi-step gradient going from 2-8% solvent B (80% acetonitrile, 20% water with 0.1% formic acid) in 5 min, 8-25% in 45 min and 25-40% in 10 min followed by a washing and an equilibration step whilst solvent A was water and 0.1% formic acid. Eluting peptides were analysed online by a coupled Q-Exactive-HF-X mass spectrometer running in data-depend acquisition mode. Full scans were performed at 120k resolution on a mass range covering 375-1500 m/z with a maxIT of 54 ms, followed by up to 8 MSMS scans at 60k resolution with a maxIT of 100 ms for up to 1e5 ions (AGC target). Precursors were isolated with a window of 1.6 m/z and fragmented with a collision energy of 30 NCE. Unassigned, singly and doubly charged features were excluded from fragmentation and dynamic exclusion was set to 10 s.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw files were converted in to mzML files using default settings in MSConvert (v 3.0.21048) from the ProteoWizard toolkit. mzML files were then analysed using Merox (v2.0.1.4.) for identification of cross-linked peptides using a single protein database of the MSP-1D protein. Overall default search parameters were used with the following changes: crosslinker was set to DSSO, lysines were set as cross-linking sites, consecutive peptides were ignored as cross-linkers and analysis mode was set to “quadratic mode” as recommended for small fasta files. A 1% false discovery rate cut-off was used including manual inspection of lower-scoring hits.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: two-stage Lys-C and trypsin digestion protocol
Characteristics[AlkylationReagent]: 30 mM iodacetamide (30 min, room temperature, in the dark)
Characteristics[Treatment]: Nonspecific cleavage and protein degradation were prevented by adding the protease inhibitors E64, leupeptin, and Pefabloc.
Comment[SpecilizedMS]: Cross-linking combined with mass spectrometry (MS) analysis
Comment[Instrument]: Q-Exactive HF-X mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[CollisionEnergy]: collision energy of 30 NCE (normalized collision energy)
Comment[Separation]: Acclaim PepMap300 C18, 5 µm, 300-Å-wide pore; Thermo Fisher Scientific
nanoEase MZ Peptide analytical column (300 Å, 1.7 µm, 75 µm by 200 mm; Waters)
